caveat: NOTE: This is a statistical cross-check, not a replacement for interpretive coding. Topic models produce word clusters, not themes.

theme.name: The Literacy Imperative: Upskilling the Newsroom for an AI Era

results.document: 2024-05-01 - Mattia Peretti Balancing AI Innovation with Journalistic Integrity.txt
results.text: It's not that everyone, every single person in a news organization needs to become an expert. in AI. I absolutely do not think that. We went through a few years ago in the journalism industry the time where everyone needed to become a coder and everyone needed to learn to code. I don't believe that's true. Definitely not with AI, but we do need to get that literacy because it's just having an impact on all of us as people in society, not just in journalism. And so we need to combine that everyone's understanding with the deeper expertise of someone in the organization, ideally not a single AI expert, but at the very least a team or a working group of people. So then those experiments that are run can immediately be understood by the newsroom so that the newsroom everyone can feed back and participate in whatever it is that an organization is trying to build.
